string.text: A standard round for the Colt M1911, IMI Desert Eagle, H&K USP 45 Compact and SIG P220 pistols. It has a standard core and a full-jacketed bullet.
A HydroShock round with improved characteristics for the Colt M1911, IMI Desert Eagle, H&K USP 45 Compact and SIG P220 pistols. It has a hollow-point bullet with a good stopping effect.

A standard 12 gauge shell filled with 6mm buckshot. It has an enormous killing power at a close range. It is designed for the TOZ-34, TOZ-66, Mossberg 88 and SPAS-12 shotguns. It is most effective at ranges under 30 meters.

A 12 gauge shell with a finned bullet. It can stabilize in flight, which increases its accuracy. It is designed for the TOZ-34, TOZ-66, Mossberg 88 and SPAS-12 shotguns. It has an armor-piercing effect and an increased range. It is effective at a range of up to 150 meters.

A 12 gauge shell with a heavy naked bullet. It is usually used for big game hunting, but can be used for other purposes. Its heavy bullet does not pierce but actually forces itself through bulletproof vests. It is designed for the TOZ-34, TOZ-66, Mossberg 88 and SPAS-12 shotguns. It is effective at a range of up to 100 meters.
An armor-piercing round for the AKS-74, AKS-74U and AN-94 Abakan assault rifles. It has an improved armor-piercing effect, but a decreased stopping effect.
A standard round for the AKS-74, AKS-74U and AN-94 Abakan assault rifles. It has a standard core and a full-jacketed bullet.
A round with an armor-piercing bullet for the Enfield L85A1, ZM LR-300-ML, SIG SG-550, H&K G36 and FN F2000 assault rifles.
A standard NATO round widely known under its Belgian SS109 index. It is used with the Enfield L85A1, ZM LR-300-ML, SIG SG-550, H&K G36 and FN F2000 assault rifles.
A cheap modification of the sniper round for the SVD and SVU sniper rifles.
A sniper round for the SVD and SVU sniper rifles. It has an improved accuracy and range.
A sniper round with an armor-piercing bullet for the SVD and SVU sniper rifles.
A standard round for the PM, PB/6P9 and Fort pistols. It has a standard core and a full-jacketed bullet. It has a weak armor-piercing effect.

A round with improved ballistic characteristics for the PM, PB/6P9 and Fort pistols. Due to the high initial velocity of its bullet, it is much more effective then a standard round. It has a better armor-piercing effect.
A standard round for the Walther P99, Beretta 92 and Browning Hi-Power pistols and the H&K MP5A1 submachine gun. It has a full-jacketed bullet with a weak armor-piercing effect.
An improved round for the Walther P99, Beretta 92 and Browning Hi-Power pistols and the H&K MP5A1 submachine gun. A high-powered round with a hollow-point bullet and an armor-piercing core.

An armor-piercing round for the OTs-14 Groza-9/40 and AS Val assault rifles and the VSS Vintorez sniper rifle. It has a subsonic bullet which due to its special design can penetrate most bulletproof vests at a range of up to 400 meters.

A round for the OTs-14 Groza-9/40 and AS Val assault rifles and the VSS Vintorez sniper rifle. The later and cheaper modification of the armor-piercing round. It has a subsonic bullet which due to its special design can penetrate most bulletproof vests at a range of up to 300 meters.

A sniper round for the OTs-14 Groza-9/40 and AS Val assault rifles and the VSS Vintorez sniper rifle. It has a subsonic bullet which due to its heavy weight and special design can penetrate most bulletproof vests at a range of up to 400 meters.
Energy cells prepared with lethal elements in a Gauss rifle magazine. The energy source is an encapsulated piece of Flash artifact.
A 40mm grenade for the Gewehraufsatz 97 grenade launcher and the integral grenade launcher of the FN F2000.
A highly-explosive 93mm rocket for the RPG-7. It is equally effective against troops and light armored vehicles.
A 40mm grenade for the GP-25 Kostyor grenade launcher, the integral grenade launcher of the OTs-14 Groza and the RG-6 revolver grenade launcher.

A 40mm grenade for the GP-25 Kostyor grenade launcher, the integral grenade launcher of the OTs-14 Groza and the RG-6 revolver grenade launcher. Prior to explosion the grenade leaps up about one meter increasing its killing effect.
A defensive hand grenade with a large radius of shrapnel damage. It is recommended to throw it only from behind cover.
A smoke grenade. Dedicated to create a smoke screen. Lasts for 10 seconds.
An offensive hand grenade with a small radius of shrapnel damage.

A folding-stock variant of the classic assault rifle, modified as a prototype replacement for the standard AKS-74. Re-chambered to fire the ubiquitous 5.45x39mm rounds found throughout the Zone, the AKMS combines time-tested Kalashnikov reliability with familiar handling, though it lacks the recoil-mitigation features of more modern designs.

Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP,
 VOG-25 and VOG-25R grenades.

A customized prototype variant of the AKMS, fitted with a high-magnification optical sight fixed directly to the receiver. Engineered as an experimental alternative to the AKS-74 for marksmen, it enables precise semi-automatic engagements at extended ranges while retaining the weapon's original compact profile.

Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP,
 VOG-25 and VOG-25R grenades.

An experimental modification of the AKMS prototype featuring a permanently integrated under-barrel grenade launcher. Intended to test heavy assault capabilities for replacing the AKS-74, this field modification turns the rifle into a versatile force-multiplier ideal for clearing out dug-in mutants and hostile stalkers.

Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP,
 VOG-25 and VOG-25R grenades.
A grenade launcher for the AKS-74 and AN-94 Abakan assault rifles.\n Ammunition:\n VOG-25 and VOG-25P grenades.
A grenade launcher for the Enfield L85A1, ZM LR-300-ML, SIG SG-550 and H&K G36 assault rifles.\n Ammunition:\n M209 grenades.
A widely used Soviet scope. The scope can be mounted on a weapon using a dovetail mount used by the Warsaw Pact countries.
A 4x scope produced in NATO countries. It can be attached to a standard NATO mount, also called the Picatinny rail.
An effective silencer made by Zone gunsmiths. The kit includes a set of adapters for weapons of any caliber.

Assault rifle with a folding butt, 1974 model, uses 5.45x39mm ammunition. It is a simple and reliable weapon, but the cheapness of its production affected its ergonomics and accuracy. In the Zone, it is the main weapon of the military stalkers and of many solo fighters.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP.

The modified closing system increases the rate of fire.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP.

The weight and size of the AKS-74U are similar to those of a submachine gun, but its operation mode and the ammunition it uses place it into the class of assault rifles. Its advantages are its small dimensions very useful when fighting inside a building or in a thick forest and a relatively high piercing capabilities. Its main disadvantages are a limited accuracy range and quick overheating when firing bursts.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP.

An enhanced machine gun. Comes with a non-removable silencer.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP.

Adopted by the American army and police, as well as many military and police units all over the world. It is also very popular among the criminals for its high-powered round and its high-capacity magazine.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x19mm FMJ,
 improved 9x19mm PB1s.

A sawn-off TOZ-66 shotgun. It is lighter and more compact then the original two-barrel shotgun, but it is effective only at a close range. One of the weapons most commonly used by beginners.
 Ammunition:
 12x70 Buckshot,
 12x76 Slug,
 12x76 Dart.

Weapon of hired assassins. Good old reliable Colt M1911, comes with a non-removable silencer.
 Ammunition:
 standard .45 ACP FMJ,
 improved .45 ACP HydroShock.

A nice gun, which was presented in all the conflicts of the 20th century with honor and entered the new century with confidence. Its small-size magazine is partially compensated by the use of a high-powered round.
 Ammunition:
 standard .45 ACP FMJ,
 improved .45 ACP HydroShock.

A real gun for real heroes. It is big, heavy and very destructive. It is not very popular in the Zone due to its price and size. After all, you don't go hunting for elephants every day!
 Ammunition:
 standard .45 ACP FMJ,
 improved .45 ACP HydroShock.

A gun that is already powerful, reworked to fit rifle bullets. Truly a killing machine for close-range combat.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x39mm PAB-9,
 armor-piercing 9x39mm SP-6,
 sniper 9x39mm SP-5.

An assault rifle combined with a grenade launcher. It is equipped with an electronic fire control module and a 40mm grenade launcher.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.56x45mm SS109,
 armor-piercing 5.56x45mm AP,
 M209 grenades.

A police pistol of Ukrainian production, replaced the PM. In comparison with the other pistols, it has improved accuracy, ergonomics and a larger magazine, but it is less reliable.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x18mm FMJ,
 improved 9x18mm +P+.

An experimental prototype of the Fort pistol.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x18mm FMJ,
 improved 9x18mm +P+.

A German assault rifle. It is a first-grade specimen of the modern weaponry: it is light, reliable, and ergonomic.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.56x45mm SS109,
 armor-piercing 5.56x45mm AP.

A super-accurate weapon made by the scientists in the Zone. It is almost noiseless, because instead of gunpowder, it uses the artifact's energy for firing rather than steam gases. In spite of the Gauss rifle’s tremendous firepower, the recoil is insignificant. The accuracy and the killing power of this gun exceed those of all other small arms.\n Ammunition:\n 2mm Gauss rounds.

An assault rifle combined with a grenade launcher. It is a very good type of assault rifle for the Zone conditions: it is compact, reliable, but universal and powerful. The OTs-14 Groza-9/40 is one of the favorite rifles of the military stalkers.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x39mm PAB-9,
 armor-piercing 9x39mm SP-6,
 sniper 9x39mm SP-5,
 VOG-25 and VOG-25P grenades.

The custom barrel is the excellent work of one of the stalker-craftsmen who managed to rework the rifle to be able to fit the more widespread 5.45x39mm round.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP,
 VOG-25 and VOG-25P grenades.

This pistol deserves to be considered as one of the best pistols of the 20th century. It is reliable, powerful and has a good grouping of shots. The magazine can hold 13 rounds.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x19mm FMJ,
 improved 9x19mm PB1s.

After the British army replaced this rifle with the German-made H&K G36, it appeared in large quantities first on the black market and then in the Zone. The Enfield L85A1’s main advantages are a standard 4x SUSAT optical sight and the high accuracy of the first shot. In burst mode, its accuracy decreases greatly, and the main mechanisms of the rifle are not reliable enough.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.56x45mm SS109,
 armor-piercing 5.56x45mm AP.

With a few modifications the magnitude of the barrel's recoil was diminished.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.56x45mm SS109,
 armor-piercing 5.56x45mm AP.

Weapon comes with an non-removable silencer. Many parts are replaced with lighter analogs, which makes the weapon significantly lighter.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.56x45mm SS109,
 armor-piercing 5.56x45mm AP.

A light rifle with an effective fire range of up to 300 meters. Due its to exceptionally easy triggering, high ergonomics and low weight, the weapon is highly accurate, though it is too sensitive to dirt and is not always suitable for use under difficult field conditions.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.56x45mm SS109,
 armor-piercing 5.56x45mm AP.

A modification of storming weaponry for firing single shots. It was assumed that it was going to be used as a cheap analog to the sniper rifle.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.56x45mm SS109,
 armor-piercing 5.56x45mm AP.

One of the best weapons in the submachine gun class. During the last decades of the 20th century it was adopted by the army and police special forces units in many countries. When it was gradually replaced with more up-to-date models, it appeared on the black market, and then in the Zone.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x19mm FMJ,
 improved 9x19mm PB1s.

Light, compact, and now redone to fit a more wide-spread caliber, this machine gun is a true find for beginning shooters.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x18mm FMJ,
 improved 9x18mm +P+.

A variation on a basic model. A non-removable silencer is attached.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x19mm FMJ,
 improved 9x19mm PB1s.

A pistol with an integral silencer, it was used by Soviet special forces. It is now outdated, but in skillful hands, still very effective.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x18mm FMJ,
 improved 9x18mm +P+.

The most widespread pistol in the Zone. It is a legacy of the Soviet era. It is relatively reliable and cheap, has low magazine capacity, low power and an unsatisfactory grouping of shots. It is the main weapon of a beginning stalker.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x18mm FMJ,
 improved 9x18mm +P+.

A revolver-type grenade launcher. Sometimes, it is just indispensable, particularly when targeting large numbers of mutants, attacking well-guarded stationary objects or fighting especially tough creatures.\n Ammunition:\n VOG-25 and VOG-25P grenades.
A variation of the revolver grenade launcher. Made especially to use grenades of the western model.\n Ammunition:\n M209 grenades.

A rocket propelled grenade launcher developed in the 1960s. Such grenade launchers are stored in large quantities in army depots, from where they are transferred to the Zone. Such a weapon is rare for stalkers: its huge dimensions and heavy weight make it unpopular, though it is indispensable for some missions.\n Ammunition:\n PG-7VL rockets.

The weapon was made for warfare in the city. To lessen the weight, some parts were removed or replaced. As a result, there is no way to attach neither a scope nor a grenade launcher under the barrel. But at least it weighs almost half of what it did before.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.56x45mm SS109,
 armor-piercing 5.56x45mm AP.

Modified for sniper fire. Furnished with a non-removable SUSAT optical sight of heightened focus.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.56x45mm SS109,
 armor-piercing 5.56x45mm AP.

A model developed back in 1975 but still popular all around the world. Its magazine has low capacity, but its mechanism is absolutely reliable. Its second advantage is the use of the high-powered .45 ACP round.
 Ammunition:
 standard .45 ACP FMJ,
 improved .45 ACP HydroShock.

A relatively new assault rifle for the 5.56x45mm round. This weapon is very popular due to its accuracy, soft recoil, and high reliability.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.56x45mm SS109,
 armor-piercing 5.56x45mm AP.

The SPAS-12 smooth-bore shotgun was developed in the late 1970th. This weapon is very reliable and flexibly used. It is however relatively big, expensive and its mechanism is too complex. Probably, this is the best close range gun in the Zone.
 Ammunition:
 12x70 Buckshot,
 12x76 Slug,
 12x76 Dart.

A unique weapon. This shotgun model has a rifled bore which greatly increases its accuracy when firing darts or slugs. However, using buckshot is more difficult.
 Ammunition:
 12x70 Buckshot,
 12x76 Slug,
 12x76 Dart.

This rifle was widely used in all operations of the Soviet and Russian armies since 1960. It proved itself to be an exceptionally reliable and handy weapon. In the Zone it is preferred by the experienced stalkers of the Duty faction which hunt mutants.
 Ammunition:
 standard 7.62x54mmR 7N1,
 armor-piercing 7.62x54mmR BP,
 sniper 7.62x54mmR 7N14.

Improved optics, and thanks to the change the resolution of the image is increased. The balance in the weapon is increased, and as a result the recoil is decreased.
 Ammunition:
 standard 7.62x54mmR 7N1,
 armor-piercing 7.62x54mmR BP,
 sniper 7.62x54mmR 7N14.

A more up-to-date version of the sniper rifle. A compact bullpup model. It is excellent for long trips in the Zone.
 Ammunition:
 standard 7.62x54mmR 7N1,
 armor-piercing 7.62x54mmR BP,
 sniper 7.62x54mmR 7N14.

This hunting rifle is popular but there’s nothing noteworthy about it.
 Ammunition:
 12x70 Buckshot,
 12x76 Slug,
 12x76 Dart.

A reliable weapon. As a quality compact close-range weapon, it is very popular among the Zone veterans.
 Ammunition:
 standard .45 ACP FMJ,
 improved .45 ACP HydroShock.

This weapon is based on the VSS Vintorez silenced sniper rifle, but it has a folding butt, can fire bursts and has a larger magazine. It was originally designed for special forces operating in conditions demanding noiseless and flameless fire.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x39mm PAB-9,
 armor-piercing 9x39mm SP-6,
 sniper 9x39mm SP-5.

Differs from the basic model in its ability to add a PSO-1 optical sight.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x39mm PAB-9,
 armor-piercing 9x39mm SP-6,
 sniper 9x39mm SP-5.

A special sniper rifle designed for noiseless and flameless sniper fire and equipped with an integral silencer. It can pierce any bulletproof vest at a range of 400 meters. It is a weapon highly valued by stalkers of any level.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x39mm PAB-9,
 armor-piercing 9x39mm SP-6,
 sniper 9x39mm SP-5.

An excellent pistol. Highly reliable with a high capacity magazine and a good fire accuracy, especially in the case of the model designed for the 9x19mm round.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x19mm FMJ,
 improved 9x19mm PB1s.

Modification of the gun for ability to use bullets of the 9x18mm caliber.
 Ammunition:
 standard 9x18mm FMJ,
 improved 9x18mm +P+.

A smooth-bore pump-action shotgun made for use in the most hostile environment. It is very reliable. All its details are covered with anticorrosive coating.
 Ammunition:
 12x70 Buckshot,
 12x76 Slug,
 12x76 Dart.

A VSS Vintorez engraved with an inscription from a stalker named Sergey Tikhiy. No one in the Zone remembers its origins—its true owner is likely long dead. This weapon possesses a remarkable property: bullets fired from it travel at a higher muzzle velocity along a flatter trajectory.

Ammunition:
 standard 9x39mm PAB-9,
 armor-piercing 9x39mm SP-6,
 sniper 9x39mm SP-5.

This pistol differs from standard models only by a donor inscription made for a prominent political figure. It remains as ergonomic and reliable as the base model. How it ended up in the Zone remains unknown, but its collector value is undoubtedly immense.

Ammunition:
 standard 9x19mm FMJ,
 improved 9x19mm PB1s.

Rumored to have been found inside a gravitational anomaly. The anomaly's influence transformed the optical glass into a diamond-like mineral analog, retaining pristine optical clarity while gaining unparalleled structural hardness.

Rumored to have been found in a pile of rusted metal at the Garbage, precisely where a gravitational anomaly had active residency for three months. Surprisingly, the AKS-74U was recovered in pristine condition, and its exposure to the anomaly granted it extraordinary properties: rounds fired from it possess significantly higher kinetic energy.

Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP.

This weapon was gifted to General Voronin by a master gunsmith in gratitude for saving his life. The OC-14 Groza underwent expert and meticulous modifications, with many internal action parts completely replaced. Thanks to these enhancements, its combat performance far surpasses that of standard production models.

Ammunition:
 standard 9x39mm PAB-9,
 armor-piercing 9x39mm SP-6,
 sniper 9x39mm SP-5.

According to stalker legends, this SVU once belonged to a veteran stalker nicknamed "Little Boy" (a man of short stature and slight build). He personally modified his rifle, significantly reducing its weight and fitting a custom recoil compensator.

Ammunition:
 standard 7.62x54mmR 7N1,
 armor-piercing 7.62x54mmR BP,
 sniper 7.62x54mmR 7N14.

Features a match-grade precision barrel crafted using cutting-edge manufacturing processes, dramatically improving weapon accuracy and terminal lethality.

Ammunition:
 standard 9x19mm FMJ,
 improved 9x19mm PB1s.

Even stock G36 models are among the most capable assault rifles in the Zone. However, this unit's performance was taken further: a member of the Monolith faction ground a "Gravi" artifact into powder and bonded it to the barrel's inner bore and outer surfaces. The result is a significant increase in muzzle velocity and precision.

Ammunition:
 standard 5.56x45mm SS109,
 armor-piercing 5.56x45mm AP.

The capacitor block of this rifle was reverse-engineered using a rare "Battery" artifact, allowing it to store significantly more electrical discharge per cell charge.

Ammunition:
 2mm Gauss batteries.

A custom-tuned version of the folding-stock rifle. This variant features a heavy match-grade barrel and an optimized gas delivery system to significantly reduce muzzle rise, resulting in noticeably tighter shot groupings during sustained automatic fire.

Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP.
A Desert Eagle bearing a custom side-plate inscription: "To the best agent of the New World."

The weapon of the elite troops in the Russian army. It was designed as a replacement for the outdated AKS-74. Not only the AN-94 Abakan’s internal mechanisms but also its receiver and its barrel are movable, which decreases the recoil and increases accuracy.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP.

Machine gun supplied with a modified PSO-1 optical sight for carrying out sniper fire. The optics embedded into the body of the weapon has a higher resolution than the standard model.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP.

This machine gun has an integral grenade launcher under the barrel, which makes this an even more universal weapon.
 Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP,
 VOG-25 and VOG-25P grenades.

A modified Maverick 88 shotgun featuring a full stock and an extended magazine tube. Recovered near gravitational anomaly fields, its internal cycling mechanism exhibits strange properties: shot pellets hit with unnatural kinetic impact as if gravity bends behind each spread, though cycling the pump action is noticeably stiffer.

Ammunition:
 12x70 Buckshot,
 12x76 Slug,
 12x76 Dart.

A compact, lightweight sniper system based on the 9A-91 rifle frame. Significantly lighter and possessing a higher fire rate than the VSS Vintorez, though its reduced mass results in stronger muzzle jump that requires skilled recoil control.

Ammunition:
 standard 9x39mm PAB-9,
 armor-piercing 9x39mm SP-6,
 sniper 9x39mm SP-5.

A classic squad automatic weapon derived from the Kalashnikov pattern. Heavier and less nimble than modern rifles found in the Zone, the RPK-74 is virtually unmatched in its ability to deliver sustained handheld suppressive fire.

Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP.

A stalker nicknamed "Lom" used to work as a machinist at an industrial metalworking plant. He applied custom tolerances to his AK-74, noticeably increasing the rifle's cyclical rate of fire.

Ammunition:
 standard 5.45x39mm FMJ,
 armor-piercing 5.45x39mm BP.

A modernized variant of the special sniper rifle designed for suppressed, flashless operations. Featuring custom modifications to the internal trigger group assembly, this model exhibits significantly reduced mechanical wear over extended firing periods.

Ammunition:
 standard 9x39mm PAB-9,
 armor-piercing 9x39mm SP-6,
 sniper 9x39mm SP-5.

A classic full-length double-barreled side-by-side hunting shotgun. Though heavy and limited to two rapid shots before reloading, its full-length barrels deliver devastating close-range power against mutants and stalkers alike. A perennial favorite among rookies and bandits.

Ammunition:
 12x70 Buckshot,
 12x76 Slug,
 12x76 Dart.
